Ukusiza ukuhamba ngokukhululeka kumingcele yayo, i-EU yashesha yenza ukusebenzisana ngezitifiketi zeCOVID-19 (ezisihloko 'EU Digital COVID-19 Certificate' noma i-'EU DCC'). Ngokwezobuchwepheshe obuvulelekile bezobuchwepheshe namazinga kuphinde kuvumele konke ukuxhumana bamazwe angekho kuEU akhipha izitifiketi ngokwe-EU DCC iziqondiso, okuba okuvulelekile kakhulu kwesixazululo esisetshenzisiwe emhlabeni wonke.
translations.reference: I am pleased that the WHO will build on the privacy-preserving principles and cutting-edge technology of the EU certificate to create a global tool against future pandemics,” added Thierry Breton, Commissioner for Internal Market. A global WHO system building on EU legacy One of the key elements in the European Union’s work against the COVID-19 pandemic has been digital COVID-19 certificates. To facilitate free movement within its borders, the EU swiftly established interoperable COVID-19 certificates (entitled ‘EU Digital COVID-19 Certificate’ or ‘EU DCC’). Based on open-source technologies and standards it allowed also for the connection of non-EU countries that issue certificates according to EU DCC specifications, becoming the most widely used solution around the world.
translations.hypothesis: I am pleased to announce that the WHO is developing a global standard for privacy and security in the use of EU digital COVID-19 certificates, which will help to create a more secure and trustworthy global travel system, according to Thierry Breton, Commissioner for External Affairs. The WHO's global standard will be based on the EU's existing digital COVID-19 certificate (also known as the 'EU Digital COVID-19 Certificate' or 'EU DCC'). In order to facilitate the smooth and secure implementation of this standard, the EU has worked closely with the WHO to develop a set of guidelines for the use of the EU DCC. These guidelines are designed to be flexible and adaptable, and they will help to ensure that the EU DCC can be used effectively in a wide range of contexts.

translations.source: Kusukela ekuqaleni kobhubhane, iWHO yakhuluma nazo zonke izifunda zeWHO ukuchaza imibandela yonke yezitifiketi. Ukusiza ukuqinisa ukuzilungiselela ezempilo komhlaba ukubhekana nokukhula kokusabisa kwezempilo, iWHO yakha uhlelokuxhumana lwesitifiketi sezempilo yedijithali yomhlaba eyakhiwa phezu kwesisekelo esiqinile yomhlahlandlela we-EU DCC, imigomo kanye nezobuchwepheshe obuvulelekile. Lokhu kubambisana iWHO izosiza loluhlelo emhlabeni ngaphansi kwesakhiwo sayo ngenhloso yokuvumela ukuthi umhlaba uhlomule ekujikeni kwezitifiketi zedijithali. Lokhu kubandakanya ukuhlela izinga nokuqinisekiswa kwemisayino yedijithali ukuze kugwenywe ukukhwabanisa. Ngokwenza njalo, iWHO ngeke ikwazi ukungena kweminye imininingo yomuntu, okuzoqhubeka kube umkhakha owehlukile wahulumeni.
translations.reference: From the onset of the pandemic, WHO engaged with all WHO Regions to define overall guidelines for such certificates. To help strengthen global health preparedness in the face of growing health threats, WHO is establishing a global digital health certification network which builds upon the solid foundations of the EU DCC framework, principles and open technologies. With this collaboration, WHO will facilitate this process globally under its own structure with the aim to allow the world to benefit from convergence of digital certificates. This includes standard-setting and validation of digital signatures to prevent fraud. In doing so, WHO will not have access to any underlying personal data, which would continue to be the exclusive domain of governments.
translations.hypothesis: Since the beginning of the pandemic, the WHO has been working with all WHO regional offices to define the requirements for all health certificates. In order to support global efforts to strengthen health preparedness and respond to the growing threat of health misinformation, the WHO has developed a global digital health certificate framework based on a robust EU DCC framework, guidelines, and open standards. This collaboration will enable the WHO to support the implementation of this framework globally, with the goal of creating a world where digital health certificates are widely accepted. This includes developing standards for the issuance and verification of digital health certificates, in order to ensure interoperability. By doing so, the WHO will help to create a more secure and trustworthy digital health ecosystem, which will benefit both individuals and governments.

translations.source: Ibhulokhu yesakhiwo sokuqala yohlelo lomhlaba lweWHO luqala ukusebenza ngoNhlangulana 2023 futhi ihlose ukuqhubeka nokudevelopha ezinyangeni ezizayo. Ukubambisana kwedijithali kwesikhathi eside ukuletha ezempilo ezingcono kuwona wonke umuntu. Ukusiza ukuthatha kwe-EU DCC yiWHO nokufaka isandla ekusebenzeni kwayo nokuqhubeka nokuthuthuka, iWHO neKhomishini yaseEurope sebevumelene ukubambisana kwezempilo yedijithali. Lokhu kubambisana kuzosebenza ukudevelopha ngezobuchwepheshe yohlelo lweWHO nokukhombisa indlela ukukhava ukusebenzisa amacala engeziwe, okungafaka, isibonelo, idijithalizeshini ye-International Certificate of Vaccination noma iProphylaxis. Ukwandisaizixazululo zedijithali ezinjalo ekwabelaneni ngokubalulekile nemigomo kuzobaluleka ukuletha ezempilo ezingcono kuzakhamizi kuwona wonke umhlaba.
translations.reference: The first building block of the global WHO system becomes operational in June 2023 and aims to be progressively developed in the coming months. A long-term digital partnership to deliver better health for all To facilitate the uptake of the EU DCC by WHO and contribute to its operation and further development, WHO and the European Commission have agreed to partner in digital health. This partnership will work to technically develop the WHO system with a staged approach to cover additional use cases, which may include, for example, the digitisation of the International Certificate of Vaccination or Prophylaxis. Expanding such digital solutions will be essential to deliver better health for citizens across the globe.
translations.hypothesis: The initial phase of the WHO's global digital health program launched in January 2023, with the aim of continuing to develop and implement innovative solutions for the future. The long-term partnership between the WHO and the EU Digital Centre for Health (DCC) is focused on improving health outcomes for all. The WHO and the European Commission have agreed to collaborate on digital health initiatives, with the goal of developing new technologies and tools, such as digital vaccination certificates or prophylactic measures. The increased use of digital health solutions will be crucial in achieving the WHO's goals of improving health outcomes for all.

translations.source: Loku kusebenza kuncike ekwabelaneni ngokubalulekile kanue nemigomo yokwethembeka nokuvuleleka, ukufaka abanye ukwenza isibopho ukuvikelwa kwemininingo nobumfihlo, ukuphepha, ukukaleka ezingeni lomhlaba nokulingana. I-WHO neKhomishi yseEurope bazosebenza ndawonye ukukhuthaza ukuthathwa komhlaba okukhulu nokubamba iqhaza. Ukunaka okuthile kuzokhokhelwa kumathuba alinganayo ekubambeni iqhaza kulabo abanesidingo amazwe anemali engenayo encane nephakathi nendawo.
translations.reference: This cooperation is based on the shared values and principles of transparency and openness, inclusiveness, accountability, data protection and privacy, security, scalability at a global level, and equity. The WHO and the European Commission will work together to encourage maximum global uptake and participation. Particular attention will be paid to equitable opportunities for the participation by those most in need: low and middle-income countries.
translations.hypothesis: The effectiveness of this approach depends on the equitable distribution of resources and the establishment of clear guidelines for data sharing, privacy protection, security, and global coordination. The WHO and the European Commission will work together to promote widespread adoption and participation. Some costs may be borne by countries with limited financial resources.

translations.source: Umhlangano weZempilo woMhlaba wesi-76 - Ukubuyekezwa kosuku: ziwu-30 kuNhlaba 2023 Umhlangano wonyaka weWHO uphela ngesivumelwano soxhasomali kanye nezinhlelo zezihloko zezempilo Umhlangano wezempilo wesi-76 weWHO uyavalwa namhlanje, nokubhekana nezinhlelo eziningi zezinkinga kanye nokuziphatha kwezesayensi; ukuthenga okuhle kwezifo okungaxhunyanwa ngazo, ukuxilonga, ukukhubazeka, ukuvikela ukuminza, okuphuthumayo nobucayi bokusebenza kokunakekelwa; izakhamzimba ezincane zokudla; ezempilo zomdabu; ukuvikelwa kokuthelelana nokulawula; ukuzala nezempilo yomntwana; umoyampilo wezokwelapha; ukunakekelwa kwezempilo kokuqala; abangenandawo nempilo yabafuduki;
translations.reference: Seventy-sixth World Health Assembly – Daily update: 30 May 2023 WHO’s annual assembly ends with agreement on funding, and array of health topics WHO’s 76th health assembly closed today, having addressed a vast array of issues, including behavioural sciences; best buys for non-communicable diseases; diagnostics; disabilities; drowning prevention; emergency, critical and operative care; food micronutrients; indigenous health; infection prevention and control; maternal and child health; medical oxygen; primary health care; refugee and migrant health;
translations.hypothesis: The 76th World Health Assembly - Day 30, November 2023: The annual WHO meeting concludes with a consensus on health priorities and strategies. Today's session of the WHO's 76th Assembly will focus on a range of global health challenges and solutions; including access to essential medicines, disease surveillance, pandemic preparedness, emergency response, and health workforce development; food security; health promotion; access to essential medicines; maternal and child health; mental health; and primary health care.

translations.source: ukuvuselelwa kwesimilo; ukwelapha ngokwesintu nosebenzi weWHO wokuphendula amadazini okuphuthumayo ngesikhathi besebenza neMember States ukuze bazilungiselele kangcono ukubhekana nezintsha. Ekuqaleni kosuku izihambeli eKomidini A zivumelene ukuqaphela umgwaqobalazwe oya kuGlobal and Peace Initiative (okuhambisana nokushintsha okuncane egameni le-"Global Health for Peace Initiative"), futhi bacela ukuthi uMqondisi Jikelele umbiko wokuqhubeka ekuqiniseni umgwaqobalazwe. Ohlelweni njengegxathu lokugcina lokugunyaza kokuhlangana, izihambeli zathatha izixazululo kanye nezinqumo kwamakomidi amabili futhi bathatha nemibiko yabo. Lokhu kufake ukugunyazwa kohlelomali luka-2024-2025 nokunyuka ngamaphesenti angu-20 okuhlolwa kokufakiwe. Izihlalo zekomidi kanye nababamele abasuka ezifundeni ezimbili bakhulumile ukubona umsebenzi nokuqhubeka kwale-Essembly.
translations.reference: rehabilitation; traditional medicine, and WHO’s work in responding to dozens of emergencies while working with Member States to be better prepared to face new ones. Earlier in the day, delegates in Committee A agreed to note the roadmap towards the Global Health and Peace Initiative (along with a slight change in name from “Global Health for Peace Initiative”), and requested that the Director-General report on progress in strengthening the roadmap. In plenary, as the final approval step of the assembly, delegates adopted the resolutions and decisions of the two committees, and adopted their reports. This included approval of the budget for 2024-25, and a 20% increase in assessed contributions. The committee chairs and representatives from two regions spoke to recognize the work and progress of this Assembly.
translations.hypothesis: The update on the global health agenda; the WHO's human and operational response to the pandemic, working with Member States to better prepare for future health emergencies. At the beginning of the session, delegates from Group A agreed to note the renaming of the Global Health for Peace Initiative (which is a slight change in the name of the "Global Health for Peace Initiative"), and requested that the Secretary-General provide a further update on the process. During the final segment of the session, delegates considered solutions and decisions from two committees and received their reports. This included the adoption of the 2024-2025 budget and a 20% increase in funding for pandemic preparedness. Delegates from two groups spoke about the work and future of the Assembly.

translations.source: Ngamazwi akhe okuvala, uDkt Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, uMqondisi jikelele weWHO uqaphele ukuthi "ukwenyuka kokubhekwa kokufakiwe kanye notshalomali kwezomlando kanye nohambo olude olukhulu." Ukhulume ngonyaka olandelayo,ngemihlangano esezingeni eliphezulu ekukhavweni kwezempilo yomhlaba, isifo sofuba nokulungiselela ubhubhane kanye nokuphendula kulonyaka ku-UN General Assembly. Ukhombe ekuqhubekeni kwezingxoxo ngokobhubhane kanye nezichibiyelo ku-International Health Regulations njengokungalindelekile-okwesizukulwane-amathuba okufunda emaphutheni obhubhane lweCOVID-19 nokuqinisekisa ukuthi awaphindi. Izithunywa ziphinde zathola ithuba lokuzwa kubasebenzi bangaphambili beWHO uGwen Carnelley, owahlanganisa iminyaka eyi-100 kulo nyaka, owaqala ukusebenza neWHO ngo-1949, ngemva nje konyaka kwasungulwa iWHO eminyakeni engu-75 edlule. Ividiyo yokuvala kwe-76 World Health Assembly.
translations.reference: In his closing remarks, Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO’s Director-General, noted that “the increase in assessed contributions and the investment round are historic and a huge milestone.” He spoke about the year ahead, with high-level meetings on universal health coverage, tuberculosis and pandemic preparedness and response at this year’s UN General Assembly. He pointed to the continuing negotiations on the pandemic accord and amendments to the International Health Regulations as unprecedented—“generational”—opportunities to learn from the mistakes of the COVID-19 pandemic and ensure they are not repeated. Delegates also had the chance to hear from former WHO staff Gwen Carnelley, who turned 100 this year, who began working with WHO in 1949, just a year after WHO was founded 75 years ago. Video of Closing of the 76th World Health Assembly.
translations.hypothesis: In his closing remarks, Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, the Director-General of WHO, emphasized the "urgent need to address the surge in misinformation and disinformation, as well as the erosion of trust in science and public health." He stated that at the upcoming World Health Assembly, to be held in the following year, discussions will focus on strengthening global health security, preventing and responding to future pandemics, and ensuring equitable access to vaccines. He also highlighted the importance of continued dialogue and collaboration under the International Health Regulations, as well as the need to build trust and resilience in the face of future health crises. Attendees had the opportunity to hear from leading WHO experts, including Dr. Gwen Carnelley, who has been with the organization for over 100 years, and who began her career with WHO in 1949, shortly after the organization was founded in 1975. The closing remarks of the 76th World Health Assembly.

translations.source: U-Gwen Carnelley wethulwe ngo-54:37 futhi wabe esekhuluma emva kwalapho. Kuqala kusasa,umhlangano wesi-153 Webhodi labaPhathi uyaqala, lapho imiphumela ye-Assembly izodingidwa kwezinye zezinto.
translations.reference: Gwen Carnelley is introduced at 54:37 and speaks shortly afterwards. Starting tomorrow, the 153rd meeting of Executive Board begins, where the outcomes of the Assembly will be discussed, among other things.
translations.hypothesis: Gwen Carnelley was dismissed at 54:37, and then proceeded to speak. Tomorrow, the 153rd session of the House of Representatives will begin, where the Assembly's findings will be discussed.

translations.source: I-World Health Assembly yamashumi ayisithupha nesithupha - Ukubuyekezwa nsuku zonke: ngo-29 kuNhlaba 2023. Isixazululo siyisikhonkwane sokuqinisa ezempilo yabantu bomdabu emhlabeni. Umhlangano weZempilo woMhlaba namhlanje ugunyaze isiqumo esingakaze sibe khona kwezempilo yebantu bomdabu, esifuna ukuthi uMqondisi Jikelele adevelophe uhlelo lokwenza lomhlaba lwezempilo yabantu bomdabu bese eyalwethula kuMhlangano weZempilo womhlaba wamashumi ayisikhombisa nesishagalolunye ngo-2026. Abantu bomdabu, yize noma bemele isibalo sabantu abehlukene kanye nemiphakathi, ngokujwayelekile kucatshangelwa abanempilo ephansi ukunokungebona abantu bomdabu. Baphinde babe nokujwayelekile okukhulu kwezifo eziningi kanye nezimo zempilo ezahlukene, okubandakanya ushukela,ukuzala kanye nokushona kwezingane kanye nokungondleki.
translations.reference: Seventy-sixth World Health Assembly – Daily update: 29 May 2023 Landmark resolution for strengthening Indigenous Peoples’ health globally The World Health Assembly today approved an unprecedented resolution on the health of Indigenous Peoples, which requests the Director-General to develop a global action plan for the health of indigenous peoples and to present it to the Seventy-ninth World Health Assembly in 2026. Indigenous Peoples, although representing diverse population groups and communities, in general have considerably lower life expectancy than non-indigenous populations. They also have a higher prevalence of many diseases and adverse health conditions, including diabetes, maternal and infant mortality and malnutrition.
translations.hypothesis: The World Health Assembly, held annually – Reviewing progress every day: November 29, 2023. This meeting represents a significant step towards improving the health of people with disabilities worldwide. The World Health Assembly today adopted a resolution that is unprecedented in the field of disability health, calling on the Director-General to develop a global strategy for disability health that will be presented at the next World Health Assembly in 2026. People with disabilities, despite their diverse backgrounds and experiences, often face significant barriers to accessing healthcare. They are also disproportionately affected by a range of health problems, including mental health issues, chronic diseases, and injuries.

translations.source: Umhlangano ucele uhlelo lokwenza ludevelophwe ngokuxhumana nabantu bomdabu; leyo WHO inikeza ukweseka kuMembers States, ngokucelwa ukwenza ngcono ezempilo zendabuko; futhi ukwenza ngcono ezempilo yaBantu boMdabu kungafakwa ekuthuthukisweni kohlelo jikelele lweWHO lomsebenzi weshuminane. Ngesixazululo esifanayo, iHealth Assembly inxuse iMember States kweminye imisebenzi ukudevelopha ulwazi ngesimo sezempilo yabantu bomdabu, ngokukhululeka kwabo, kanye nokunikeza imvume; ukudevelopha, ukuxhasa nokwenza izinhlelo zempilo zezwe,amasu kanye nezinye izinyathelo zabantu bomdabu;
translations.reference: The Assembly requested the action plan be developed in consultation with Indigenous Peoples; that WHO provide support to Member States, upon request, for improving indigenous health; and that the improvement of Indigenous Peoples’ health be included in the development of the Fourteenth WHO General Programme of Work. In the same resolution, the Health Assembly urged Member States to, among other tasks, develop knowledge about the health situation of Indigenous Peoples, with their free, prior and informed consent; develop, fund and implement national health plans, strategies or other measures for Indigenous Peoples;
translations.hypothesis: The assembly called for the development of a comprehensive plan to improve the health of indigenous peoples, in collaboration with indigenous communities; and for the WHO to provide support to Member States in this effort, as well as to integrate improved indigenous health into the overall WHO strategy for health cooperation. In a similar vein, the Health Assembly urged Member States to develop their own strategies for improving the health of indigenous peoples, including collecting data on their health status, promoting their rights, and implementing appropriate health programs and policies.

translations.source: ukugqugquzela ukuheha, ukuqeqesha, ukuqasha nokuphinda kubantu bomdabu njengabasebenzi bezempilo uphinde ucabange ulwazi lwesintu kanye nokwenza. Idokhumenti ehlobene: A76/A/CONF./1 - Ezempilo zaBantu bomdabu. Isixaululo sokuqala ukuqhuba ukwenza ekuvikelweni kokuminza, izihambeli eMhlanganweni weZempilo woMhlaba wamashumi ayisithupha nesithupha basivuma isixazululo sokuqhuba ukwenza ekuvikeleni ukuminza emhlabeni. Isicelo sesixazululo seMember States ukubheka isimo sabo sokuminza kanye nokudevelopha nokwenza ngemikhakha eminingi izinhlelo zokuvikela ukuminza. Okudala ukuminza ukufa okungu-236 000 njalo ngonyaka.
translations.reference: encourage the attraction, training, recruitment and retention of Indigenous Peoples as health workers taking into account the traditional knowledge and practices. Related document: A76/A/CONF./1 - Health of Indigenous Peoples First-ever resolution to accelerate action on drowning preventionThe delegates at the Seventy-sixth World Health Assembly agreed a resolution to accelerate action on global drowning prevention. The resolution requests Member States to assess their national drowning situation and to develop and implement multisectoral drowning prevention programmes. Drowning causes 236 000 deaths every year.
translations.hypothesis: Promoting health, training, and providing support to vulnerable populations, such as healthcare workers, and raising awareness about human rights and well-being. Relevant documents: A76/A/CONF./1 - Health of Vulnerable Populations. The first step in implementing a global strategy for protecting vulnerable populations is to recognize the importance of health and well-being. The Member States have agreed to a global strategy for protecting vulnerable populations, which includes a commitment to implement a comprehensive approach to health and well-being. This strategy aims to reduce the number of preventable deaths to 236,000 per year.

translations.source: Isizathu somhlaba esihamba phambili kokufa kwezingane okuhlobene nokulimala. Kushuminyaka eledlule, abantu abayizigidi ezingu-2.5 bafa ngenxa yokuminza futhi ngaphezu kuka-90% walokho kwenzeka emazweni anemali engenayo encane kuya kwephakathi nendawo. Esimemweni se-United Nations (UN) General Assembly, iWHO izoxhumanisa izenzo kuhlelo lweUN lokuvikela ukuminza nokusiza ukunaka uSuku lokuVikela ukuMinza eMhlabeni ngomhlaka-25 kuNtulikazi njalo ngonyaka. I-WHO izophinde ihlele iGlobal Alliance yokuvikela ukuminza nezinhlangano zohlelo lwe-UN, ababambisani bokuthuthukisa amazwe angaphandle kanye nezinhlangano ezizimele. Ukuqonda kangcono umthwalo wangempela nomthelela wokuminza, isixazululo zezicelo kuWHO ukulungiselela umbiko wesimo samhlaba ekuvikeleni ukuminza.
translations.reference: It is a leading global cause of injury-related child deaths. Over the past decade, 2.5 million people died from drowning, and over 90% of those occurred in low- and middle-income countries. At the invitation of the United Nations (UN) General Assembly, WHO will coordinate actions within the UN system on drowning prevention and facilitate the observance of World Drowning Prevention Day on 25 July each year. WHO will also set up a Global Alliance for Drowning Prevention with organizations of the UN system, international development partners and nongovernmental organizations. To better understand the true burden and impact of drowning, the resolution further requests WHO to prepare a global status report on drowning prevention.
translations.hypothesis: The leading cause of death for children worldwide is injury. In the past year, over 2.5 million children died from injuries, and more than 90% of these deaths occurred in low- and middle-income countries. At the United Nations (UN) General Assembly, the WHO will present proposals for integrating injury prevention into the UN's Sustainable Development Goals. The WHO will also work with the UN system, partner organizations, and civil society to promote injury prevention efforts. By better understanding the true burden of injury, the WHO is seeking to inform the development of a global strategy for injury prevention.

translations.source: Amadokhumentu ahlobene: EB152/CONF./2 - Ukuqhuba ukwenza ngokuvikela ukuminza emhlabeni Funda okwengeziwe ngesixazululo Usuku lokuvikela ukuminza emhlabeni 2023 I-Member States ihlanganise isinqumo ngemuva kokubhekana namakhemikhali, udoti kanye nokungcola, ama-Member State amukele isinqumo sokubhekana nabazinikele bendawo, okufana nokuphathwa kwamakhemikhali kanye nodoti. Imizamo ingasiza ukuvikela kuze kufike ngokufa okuhlobene nokuzibulala okuvela kuzibulalanambuzane.
translations.reference: Related document: EB152/CONF./2 - Accelerating action on global drowning prevention Read more about the resolution World Drowning Prevention Day 2023 Member States mobilize behind resolution to tackle chemicals, waste and pollution Member States welcomed the resolution addressing environmental determinants, including management of chemicals and waste. The efforts can help prevent up to one fifth of all suicide-related deaths from highly hazardous pesticides.
translations.hypothesis: Relevant documents: EB152/CONF./2 - Promoting safe and sustainable agriculture. For more information, see the 2023 World Environment Day resolution. The Member States have adopted a resolution following discussions on chemicals, pesticides, and other environmental hazards. This resolution includes measures to address the risks associated with agricultural practices. These measures can help to mitigate the risks of self-harm caused by exposure to agricultural chemicals.

translations.source: Ama-Member State ayanxenxwa ukuba ibe yisezeseko sokwenza samasu akhona eWHO okufaka ukwenza kweMgwaqobalazwe lwamakhemikhali eWHO eveza izindima ezibalulekile emkhakheni wezempilo ekwenzeni Indlela yeSu eKuphathweni kwamaKhemikhali eMazweni angaphandle. Isixazululo siphinde sigqugquzele uNgqongqoshe wezeMpilo ukuthi akhulume emizamweni ukulungiselela iziphakamiso kuphaneli yesayensi yenqubomgomo yangaphakathi kohulumeni kanye nezingxoxo kwesivumelwano sokuqeda ukugcola koshekhasi. Isixazululo sibizwe ngoMqondisi Jikelele ukuthatha izinqumo ezimbalwa, okufaka: Ukushicilela umbiko ezinkingeni zempilo yamakhemikhali, udoti nokungcola ngendlela ye-"One Health"; ukubuyekezwa kwedokhumenti yango-2012 kuState yeScience Of Endocrine Disrupting Chemicals ngokuhlanganyela ne-United NationsEnvironment Programme; futhi
translations.reference: Member States were urged to bolster the implementation of existing WHO strategies including implementation of the WHO Chemicals Roadmap which outlines key roles of the health sector in implementing the Strategic Approach to International Chemicals Management. The resolution also encourages the Ministries of Health to engage in efforts to prepare proposals for an intergovernmental science policy panel and negotiations for a treaty to end plastic pollution. The resolution called upon the Director-General to undertake several actions, including: publishing a report on the health implications of chemicals, waste and pollution from a "One Health" perspective; updating the 2012 document on the State of the Science of Endocrine Disrupting Chemicals jointly with the United Nations Environment Programme; and
translations.hypothesis: The Member States agree that the WHO should be a leading body in developing guidelines for the safe use of chemicals, including those related to the WHO's Programme on Chemicals and Waste Management in Developing Countries. The solution also calls on the Minister of Health to advocate for the inclusion of these recommendations in the government's science advisory panel and in discussions on the implementation of the Global Treaty to End Plastic Pollution. The solution is led by the Director-General, who will take the following actions: publish a report on the health impacts of chemicals, pesticides, and plastic pollution; review the 2012 State of the Science of Endocrine Disrupting Chemicals report in collaboration with the United Nations Environment Programme; and

translations.source: ukweseka amazwe ezweni elithuthukayo noma izinhlelo zokubheka abantu besifunda ngokukhathazeka ngamakhemikhali. idokhumenti ehlobene: A76/A/CONF./2 - Umthelela wamakhemikhali,udoti nokungcola kwezempilo yomuntu. Umsebenzi weWHO ekuphepheni kwekhemikhali nokuthola ezempilo nokuphila: umhlahlandlela womhlaba weMember States uvumile ukuthatha umhlahlandlela womhlaba ekuhlanganiseni ukuphila ekusebenziseni kwezempilo zomphakathi indlela yokuqguqguzela ezempilo, okuhlose ukwenza bantu bonke baphumelele futhi bathole okwesiqu sabo konke namandla ezempilo yengqondo ngezimpilo zabo kusona sonke isizukulwane. Umhlahlandlela womhlaba uphakamise izindlela zamasu abalulekile ayisithupha okuzogxilwa kuwona: ukukhavwa kwezempilo emhlabeni,ezomnotho ezilinganayo ukuvikela umhlaba,izinhlelo zokuvikela ezenhlalakahle, izinhlelo zedijithali ukusiza ezempilo ukukala nokubheka ukuphila.
translations.reference: supporting countries in developing national or regional human biomonitoring programmes for chemicals of concern. Related document: A76/A/CONF./2 - The impact of chemicals, waste and pollution on human health WHO’s work on chemical safety and health Achieving well-being: a global frameworkMember States agreed to adopt the "Global framework for integrating well-being into public health utilizing a health promotion approach", which strives to enable all people to flourish and achieve their full physical and mental health potential throughout their lives and across generations. The Global framework recommends six key strategic directions that focus on: universal health coverage, equitable economies, protecting the planet, social protection systems, digital systems to enable health, and measuring and monitoring well-being.
translations.hypothesis: Protecting countries from emerging or existing threats posed by chemicals. Relevant documents: A76/A/CONF./2 - The role of chemicals, drugs and health in sustainable development. The WHO's role in chemical safety and health: a global framework for member states to adopt a global approach to chemical safety, with the aim of ensuring that all people have access to safe and healthy environments, and that they are able to enjoy their full potential in all aspects of life. The global framework promotes a number of key priorities, including: promoting sustainable development, protecting the environment, ensuring access to safe and healthy environments, and promoting the use of safe and healthy technologies.

translations.source: Izikphakamiso somhlahlendlela zokubambisana okusondelene nemikhakha ngaphandle komkhakha wezempilo ukugqugquzela nokuvikela okwezempilo. Kusiza njengomyalelo wabobonke ababambisani ukuthi bakhulume ngokuxhumana nokubambisana ngenhloso efanayo: ukugqugquzela ezempilo zabantu kanye nomhlaba ngendlela egcinekile nelinganayo. ukugqugquzela ezempilo yabantu emhlabeni ngendlela egcinekile nelinganayo. Umhlangano ucela uMqondisi Jikelele ukuthi abike ukwenza komhlahlandlela womhlaba ngo-2024, 2026 kanye no-2031. Idokhumenti ehlobene: A76/7 Add.2- ukuphila nokugqugquzela impilo
translations.reference: The framework proposes close collaboration with sectors outside the health sector to promote and protect health. It serves as a guide for all stakeholders to engage in a coherent and coordinated manner around a common purpose: promoting the health of people and planet in a sustainable and equitable manner. promoting the health of people and planet in a sustainable and equitable manner. The Assembly requests the Director-General to report on implementation of the global framework in 2024, 2026 and 2031. Related document: A76/7 Add.2 - Well-being and health promotion
translations.hypothesis: Recommendations for collaborative initiatives outside the health sector to promote and protect public health. These provide a framework for all partners to communicate and collaborate towards a common goal: to improve the health and well-being of people and the environment in a sustainable and equitable way. The meeting requests the Secretary-General to initiate a global dialogue on this topic in 2024, 2026, and 2031. Relevant document: A76/7 Add.2 - Health and well-being

translations.source: Isixazululo esisha sokuqhuba imizamo ekuqinisekisweni kokudla okunomsoco omncane. Izihambeli zigunyaze isiphakamiso sokuqhuba imizamo nokuvikela ukushoda komsoco omncane ngokuphepha kanye nokuqinisekiswa kokudla okufanele. Ukushoda kumavithamini kanye nesimo samaminerali,ikakhulukazi ifolathi,ayoni,uvithamin A nozinki, unomthelela ka-50% ezinganeni zonke ezisenkulisa kanye no-67% wabo bonke abantu besifazane abaseminyakeni yokuzala emhlabeni wonke. ukushoda kokudla okuncane kungaba nemiphumela yangempela okufaka isipina bifida kanye namashubhu okuhlola isikali esikhulu sokuqinisekisa ukudla njengengxenye yesixazululo. Ngokwengeza amavithamini abalulekile namaminerali ukuhlanganisa ukudla namakhondimenti njengokolweni impiphu ufulawa, ilayisi,amafutha nosawoti ngokuvumelana namaphethini okugcina kwezwe kanye nokushoda,amazwe angalungisa nokuqhubeka ukuvikela ukushoda kwezakhamuzimba ezincane ezikhonjiswayo.
translations.reference: New resolution to accelerate efforts on food micronutrient fortification The delegates approved a resolution on accelerating efforts to prevent micronutrient deficiencies through safe and effective food fortification. Deficiencies in vitamin and mineral status, particularly of folate, iron, vitamin A, and zinc, affect 50% of all preschool aged children and 67% of all women of reproductive age worldwide. Micronutrient deficiencies can have serious consequences, including spina bifida and other neural tube defects.Large scale food fortification is part of the solution. By adding essential vitamins and minerals to staple foods and condiments, such as wheat and maize flours, rice, cooking oil and salt in accordance with national consumption patterns and deficiencies, countries can correct and further prevent a demonstrated micronutrient deficiency.
translations.hypothesis: A new approach to promoting efforts to ensure adequate intake of essential micronutrients. The initiative recommends focusing on safe and reliable methods for obtaining essential micronutrients. Deficiencies in vitamins and minerals, particularly iron, folate, vitamin A, and zinc, affect 50% of all children and 67% of all women of childbearing age worldwide. Micronutrient deficiencies can have serious consequences, including impaired cognitive development and increased susceptibility to illness. As part of the solution, the initiative emphasizes the importance of incorporating nutrient-rich foods into diets, such as fruits, vegetables, whole grains, and lean protein sources, while also addressing underlying factors that contribute to micronutrient deficiencies, such as poverty and lack of access to healthcare.

translations.source: Isixazululo sinxenxa iMember States ukuthi idevelophe izinqubomgomo ekuqinisekisweni kokudla okunomsoco omncane noma izikhuthazi, nokucabanga izindlela zokuqinisa ezezimali nokubheka indlela yokwenza. Isixazululo kwakuvunyelwene ngaso ngaphansi kwesambulela seUnited Nations of Action of Nutrition (2016-2025) umbiko. idokhumenti ehlobene: EB152/CONF./5 Ukuqhuba imizamo yokuvikela ukushoda komsoco omncane kanye nemiphumela yakhona, okufaka isphina bifida kanye nezinye amashubhu okuhlola, ngokuphepha nokuqinisekisa ukudla okufanele Amalinki axhumene
translations.reference: The resolution urges Member States to develop policies on food fortification with micronutrients and/or supplementation, and to consider ways of strengthening financing and monitoring mechanisms. The resolution was agreed under the umbrella of the United Nations Decade of Action on Nutrition (2016-2025) report. Related document: EB152/CONF./5 Accelerating efforts for preventing micronutrient deficiencies and their consequences, including spina bifida and other neural tube defects, through safe and effective food fortification Related links
translations.hypothesis: The solution examines how Member States can develop policies to promote affordable and nutritious food, and to explore ways to strengthen food security and resilience. This solution was developed in accordance with the United Nations' 2016-2025 Global Action Plan on Nutrition. Relevant document: EB152/CONF./5. Efforts to address malnutrition and its consequences, including food insecurity and undernutrition, are being undertaken in a safe and effective manner.

translations.source: Funda okwengeziwe ngesixazululo Umsebenzi weWHO ekuqinisekisweni kokudla Ama-Member State agunyaza izenzo ukubhekana namazinga nemithi engalungile. Ngomhlaka-27 Nhlaba, Umhlangano weZempilo yoMhlaba wamashumi ayisithupha agunyaze isinqumo sokwenza ukubheka okuzimele seMember States Mechanism (MSM) yezingozi, okungaphansi kanye nemithi yemigunyathi, kugcizelela uMqondisi Jikelele ngoMasingana 2023 isimemezelo sokunyusan ukuqaphela nokwenza kulendawo ebalulekile. Ukubukisiswa kuzoqalwa ngomhlangano olandelayo weMSM ekupheleni kuka-2023.
translations.reference: Read more about the resolution WHO's work on food fortification Member States approve actions to counter substandard and falsified medicines On 27 May, the Seventy-sixth World Health Assembly approved a resolution to conduct an independent review of the Member State Mechanism (MSM) for incidents, substandard and falsified medicines, reinforcing the Director-General’s January 2023 call for increased vigilance and action in this important area. The review will be initiated at the next MSM steering committee meeting later in 2023.
translations.hypothesis: Learn more about the solution: The World Health Organization (WHO) is working with Member States to strengthen food safety. On November 27, the World Health Assembly adopted a resolution to establish a dedicated mechanism for Member States to identify and address food safety risks, including those related to pesticides. The mechanism will be led by the Director-General of the WHO and will be operational by the end of 2023.

translations.source: Umhlangano wasungula iMSM ngo-2012 ukubhekana namazinga kanye nemikhiqizo yezokwelapha yamanga ngendlela yezempilo yomphakathi nangendlela enokuthembeka neyangaphakathi. Inhloso yamaMSM ukuvikela ezempilo yomphakathi nokugqugquzela ukuthola okukhonekayo, okuphephile amandla okwenza kanye nokulingana kwemikhiqizo yokwelapha. Ukuqaphela izigigaba zakamuva zemithi engcolile etholakale emakathe ehlukene nokuhlobana kokuvikela ukufa, iMember States Igcizelele ukweseka okudingekayo ukwenzangcono amandla abo ukuvikela,ukuthola nokuphendula kokungaphansi nemikhiqizo yezokwelapha engumgunyathi ngokuthola ezobuchwepheshe okufanele, okufaka izindawo zokuhlola elebhu, okuhambisana nokubheka okwenziwe ngcono ezimakethe ezingekho emthethweni nokukhangisa,ukudayisa ku-inthanethi kwemikhiqizo yezokwelapha. I-Member States ibizele ukwamukela nokwenza ubuchwepheshe bokulandelela nokubheka ukulawula imakethe nokubheka kanye nesidingo sokubambisana okusemthethweni ngabahlobene bendawo nababambisani bendawo njengabomthetho nokuphathwa wemikhuba. I-Member States igqugquzelwe ukuhambisana nezivumelwano ezihlose ukwenzangcono umthetho kanye nezikhundla zamasanishini angavunyelwe ngokwenza okungafanele emazingeni kanye nemikhiqizo yezokulapha eyamanga.
translations.reference: The Assembly established the MSM in 2012 to tackle substandard and falsified medical products from a public health perspective and in a transparent and inclusive way. The goal of the MSM is to protect public health and promote access to affordable, safe, efficacious and quality medical products. Noting recent events of contaminated medicines found on various markets and related preventable deaths, Member States emphasized the support needed to improve their capacity to prevent, detect and respond to substandard and falsified medical products through access to appropriate technologies, including laboratory testing facilities, along with improved oversight on informal markets and advertising, and the online sale of medical products. Member States called for embracing and implementing track and trace technologies for market control and surveillance, as well as the need for formal collaboration by relevant local and global partners such as law enforcement and customs administrations. Member States were encouraged to align with conventions aimed at improvement of legislation and imposition of prohibitive sanctions for wrongful dealing in substandard and falsified medical products.
translations.hypothesis: The MSM was established in 2012 to address the issues of substandard and counterfeit medical products through a public health and safety approach. The primary goal of the MSM is to protect public health and safety by identifying, preventing, and responding to the proliferation of substandard and counterfeit medical products. In light of recent reports of counterfeit medicines circulating in various markets, the Member States have emphasized the need to strengthen their capacity to detect, prevent, and respond to the threat of substandard and counterfeit medical products. The Member States have also called for the development and implementation of appropriate standards and regulations for the testing and evaluation of medical products, including laboratory testing, market surveillance, and product labeling. The Member States have also encouraged cooperation among Member States and with other relevant stakeholders, such as law enforcement agencies and regulatory bodies, to combat the proliferation of substandard and counterfeit medical products. The Member States are committed to working together to strengthen the legal and regulatory framework for the control of substandard and counterfeit medical products.

translations.source: Unobhala ucele umbiko ngokuqhubekayo kulendawo ku-Executive Board ngo-2025. Idokhumenti ehlobene: EB152/9 - Amazinga kanye nemikhiqizo yezokwelapha engamanga. Amalinki axhumene Indlelaye yeWHO Member State ezinkingeni, amazinga kanye nemikhiqizo yezokulapha okungeyiyo. Umsebenzi weWHO kumazinga nemikhiqizo yekwelapha okwamanga.
translations.reference: The Secretariat is requested to report on progress in this area to the Executive Board in 2025. Related document: EB152/9 - Substandard and falsified medical products Related links WHO Member State Mechanism on incidents, substandard and falsified medical products WHO's work on substandard and falsified medical products
translations.hypothesis: The proposal requests an update on the ongoing work of the Executive Board in 2025. Relevant documents: EB152/9 - Innovative and essential medicines. Links to WHO Member State reports on issues, standards, and regulations related to essential medicines. The WHO's work on innovative and essential medicines.

translations.source: Isixazululo sokuqala samasayensi ezokuziphatha kwezempilo engcono iyathathwa. Namhlanje iMember States yamukele ukuziphatha kwamasayensizi kwesinqumo sezempilo esingcono neMember States okukhombisa isivumelwano esikhulu esiya kusidingo sokuhlanganisa ngokohlelo lwesayensi yokuziphatha injulalwazi,izindlela naye namaqhinga kuzona zonke izihloko zempilo nokusebenza kwezempilo zomphakathi. Isinqumo sinxenxa iMember States ukuthi ibone indima yesayensi yokuziphatha ekutholeni imiphumela yezempilo engcono amathuba okuhlonza kokunyusa ukusebenzisa nokwakha imisebenzi namayunithi okwakha nokuhumusha ubufakazi ukwazisa izinqubomgomo kanye nezinhlelo. Iphinde icele uMqondisi Jikelele ukuthi aveze ukusebenzisa isayensi yokuziphatha kunhlangano nokunikeza ukweseka iMember States ngokuthuthukisa ukulawulwa nokunikezwa usizo lwezobuchwepheshe. I-Member States ibone ukuthola kweSayensi yokuziphatha yomzamo wezempilo engcono eholwa ngonobhala, uhalalisele iMalaysia ngokuxhasa isinqumo kanye namanye amazwe angu-19 ajoyine abaxhasi futhi wabonga umbiko kaMqondisi Jikelele.
translations.reference: First ever resolution on behavioural sciences for better health is adopted Today Member States adopted the behavioural sciences for better health resolution with Member States showing broad consensus towards the need for integrating systematically behavioural science theory, methods and approaches across health topics and public health functions. The resolution urges Member States to acknowledge the role of behavioural science in achieving better health outcomes, to identify opportunities for increased use and, to establish functions and units for generating and translating evidence to inform policies and programmes. It also requests the Director-General to mainstream the use of behavioural science within the organization and to provide support to Member States through the development of guidance and the provision of technical assistance. Member States recognized the achievement of the Behavioural Sciences for Better Health Initiative led by the secretariat, congratulated Malaysia, sponsor of the resolution, as well as the other 19 countries that joined as co-sponsors, and thanked the Director-General for the report.
translations.hypothesis: The first step towards better public health is to establish a strong and independent body of scientific experts. Today, the Member States have agreed to create a new body of scientific experts in public health, which will provide independent advice and guidance to policymakers on all aspects of public health and healthcare. The body will be made up of experts from a variety of fields, including medicine, epidemiology, and public health. It will also be independent of government and other vested interests. The body will be responsible for providing evidence-based advice to policymakers on a wide range of issues, including disease prevention, treatment, and health promotion. It will also be responsible for monitoring and evaluating the effectiveness of public health programs and policies. The Member States have also agreed to provide the body with the resources it needs to carry out its work, including funding, staff, and access to data. The Member States have also agreed to cooperate with each other in the development and implementation of the body's work. The Member States have expressed their support for the new body and have pledged to work together to ensure its success.

translations.source: Ukubaluleka okugcizeleliwe kwamandla okwakha kulendawo, ikakhulukazi amahhovisi esifunda nokwakha inqolobane yobufakazi, kanye nezinto phakathi kwemikhakha okufaka nezemfundo nomkhakha ozimele. Idokhumenti ehlobene: EB152(23) - Amasayensi okuziphatha kwezempilo engcono EB152/25 - isayensi yokuziphatha yezempilo engcono, umbiko nguMqondisi Jikelele Isayensi yokuziphatha yempilo engcono yokuqaliwe.izinto ezingaphezulu zadingidwa njengengxenye yedokhumenti A76/7 Rev.1 - Umbiko ohlanganisiswe nguMqondisi Jikelele.
translations.reference: They stressed the importance of building capacity in this area, particularly in regional offices, and of creating a repository of evidence and synergies between sectors, including with academia and the private sector. Related documents: EB152(23) - Behavioural sciences for better health EB152/25 - Behavioural sciences for better health, Report by the Director-General Behavioural Sciences for Better Health Initiative The above items were discussed as part of the document A76/7 Rev.1 - Consolidated report by the Director-General.
translations.hypothesis: The importance of building sustainable infrastructure, particularly schools and public buildings, as well as facilities for various sectors including education and commerce, is highlighted. Relevant documents include: EB152(23) - Guidelines for Healthy Building Practices; EB152/25 - Guidelines for Healthy Building Practices, issued by the General Manager; and A76/7 Rev.1 - Report prepared by the General Manager.

translations.source: Umhlangano weZempilo woMhlaba wamashumi ayisithupha nesithupha - ukubuyekezwa kwansukuzonke: ngo-27 kuNhlaba 2023. Isu lokuqala lomhlaba lokuvikela kokuthelelana nokulawula uMhlangano weZempilo woMhlaba uvumile namhlanje ukuthi isu lomhlaba lokuqala ngqa ekuthelelaneno nokubavikela nokulawula (IPC) Okwakha cishe amashumi nyaka amabili yemizamo eholwa yiWHO nababambisani,. Isu linikeza iMember States ngezindlela zamasu zokugcineka kokwehlisa ubungcuphe obuqhubekayo kwezokunakekelwa kwezempilo okuhlobene nokuthelelana(HAIs) okufaka leyo mibukiso yokuvimba ama-antimicrobial. Ama-HAI akweminye imicimbi eyahlukene ejwayeleke kakhulu eyenzeka kungqikithi yokulethwa kwemisebenzi yezempilo. Ubhubhane lweCOVID-19 nokubheduka kwezifo ezinkulu kamuva nje njengesifo segciwane i-Ebola, isifo sokuphefumula saseMiddle East nesifo segciwane eSudan sekuveze ngokusobala izikhala kuzinhlelo ze-IPC kuwona wonke amazwe.
translations.reference: Seventy-sixth World Health Assembly – Daily update: 27 May 2023 First global strategy on infection prevention and controlThe World Health Assembly agreed today on the first-ever global strategy on infection prevention and control (IPC), which builds on almost two decades of efforts led by WHO and partners. The strategy provides Member States with strategic directions to substantially reduce the ongoing risk of health care-associated infections (HAIs), including those that exhibit antimicrobial resistance. HAIs are among the most frequent adverse events occurring in the context of health service delivery. The COVID-19 pandemic and recent major disease outbreaks such as Ebola virus disease, the Middle East respiratory syndrome and the Sudan virus disease have clearly exposed the existing gaps in IPC programmes in all countries.
translations.hypothesis: The World Health Organization's (WHO) first global meeting on infection prevention and control was held on November 27, 2023. This meeting, the first of its kind, brought together representatives from WHO and its partners to discuss the urgent need for a coordinated global response to the growing problem of healthcare-associated infections (HAIs). The meeting provided Member States with a framework for developing national strategies to prevent and control HAIs, including the use of evidence-based interventions such as hand hygiene and antimicrobial stewardship programs. HAIs are a major public health concern, and they are responsible for a significant number of deaths and hospitalizations each year. The COVID-19 pandemic and the recent outbreaks of diseases such as Ebola, Middle East Respiratory Syndrome (MERS), and Sudan ebolavirus have further highlighted the importance of infection prevention and control in all healthcare settings.

translations.source: Isu liveza umbono ocacile: ngo-2030 wonke umuntu oyothola noma ukunakekelwa kwezempilo okuphephile okusuka nokuhlobene nokutheleleka. Izinjongo ezintathu ezibalulekile: ukuvikela ukuthelelana kwezokunakekelwa kwezempilo; isenzo sokuqinisekisa izinhlelo ze-IPC ezikhona nokwenza nokuxhumana nemisebenzi ye-IPC kwezinye izindawo kanye nemikhakha. Isu ligxile kunoma yikuphi ukuhlela lapho ukunakekelwa kwezempilo kulethwa, kuzona zonke izinhlelo zempilo, ligxile kumgomo wokuhlanzeka nokuphepha njengezinto ezibalulekile kubulungiswa bezempilo okulethwa ngokulingana futhi kuzoqinisekisa ukuzibophezela nokugcineka. Isu lomhlaba le-IPC lizohambisana noma lizosetshenziswa ngokuhlanganisa noma ukuhlobana nesu lokwenza lomhlaba nomhlahlandlela wokubheka,ozodevelophwa ngo-2023-2024. Idokhumenti ehlobene EB152/9 - Isu lomzamo womhlaba ekuvikelweni kokuthelelana nokulawula, okugoqiwe kombiko we-Executive nguMqondisi Jikelele.
translations.reference: The strategy sets a clear vision: by 2030, everyone accessing or providing health care is safe from associated infections. Its three key objectives are: to prevent infection in health care; act to ensure IPC programmes are in place and implemented; and coordinate IPC activities with other areas and sectors. The strategy is focused on any setting where health care is delivered, across the health system; it is based on the principle of clean and safe care as a fundamental component of the right to health, which is equity driven, and which should ensure accountability and sustainability. The global IPC strategy will be complemented by, and used in conjunction with, an associated global action plan and monitoring framework, that will be developed in 2023–2024. Related document EB152/9 - Draft global strategy on infection prevention and control, Executive summary Report by the Director-General
translations.hypothesis: ISU presents a clear vision: by 2030, every person will have access to safe and reliable healthcare services. Three key objectives: to protect the integrity of healthcare services; to ensure the effectiveness of existing IPC systems and to develop and implement new IPC programs in other settings and sectors. ISU is committed to any planning that involves healthcare delivery, across all settings, with a focus on cleanliness and safety as essential components of quality healthcare delivery and ensuring accountability and sustainability. The global IPC strategy will align with or complement the global and national frameworks for healthcare, which will be developed by 2023-2024. The document related to EB152/9 - Global IPC strategy for protection and control, drafted by the Executive Director.

translations.source: Umsebenzi weWHO ekuvikeleni nokulawula ukutheleleka,kuyisinqumo esingumlando esivula indlela yokuqinisa ukuhlumelelisa isimilo kuzinhlelo zempilo namhlanje eMhlanganweni weZempilo woMhlaba kuvunyelenwe ngesikhonkwane sesinqumo ekuqiniseni ukuhlunyeleliswa kwezinhlelo zempilo. Imisebenzi yokuhlumelelisa isimilo idlala indima enkulu ukuqinisekisa ukujabulela amalungelo abantu okufaka izinga elitholakalayo eliphezulu lesiqu kanye nezempilo zengqondo. Kuphinde kugqugquzele ezocansi nezempilo yokuzala, kubona ilungelo lokusebenza kanye nelungelo lezemfundo. Lesi sinqumo sesikhonkwane ihlose ukubhekana nezingqinamba yokuhlumelelisa isimilo ngendlela edingeka ngayo: ukunyusa ukugqugquzela kokuhlunyeleliswa kwesimilo uma kuhlelwa okuhamba phambili kwezempilo kanye nama-ajenda okucwaninga, ukufakwa kwezinsizakusebnza, ukugqugquzela ukusebenzisana nokuvumela ukudluliswela kwezobuchwepheshe;
translations.reference: WHO's work on infection prevention and control Historic resolution paves the way for strengthening rehabilitation in health systemsToday the World Health Assembly agreed a landmark resolution on strengthening rehabilitation in health systems. Rehabilitation services play a key role in ensuring the enjoyment of human rights including the highest attainable standard of physical and mental health. It also promotes sexual and reproductive health, and recognizes the right to work and the right to education. This landmark resolution aims to address the challenges in rehabilitation such as the need to: increase awareness of rehabilitation when setting health priorities and research agendas, allocating resources, promoting cooperation and enabling technology transfer;
translations.hypothesis: The WHO's work in protecting and controlling infectious diseases is a historic decision that has paved the way for strengthening public health systems today. The focus on disease prevention plays a crucial role in ensuring the enjoyment of human rights, including the right to a safe and healthy environment. It also supports economic and social development, as well as the right to education and employment. This decision reflects the recognition that infectious diseases pose a significant threat to global health and well-being, and that a coordinated and comprehensive approach is needed to address this challenge.

translations.source: ukuqinisekisa amazwe acijiswe kangcono ukuphendula ngokusesha kokunyuka ezidingweni zokuhlunyeleliswa kwesimilo okufaka ukusiza kobuchwepheshe ngenxa yokuphuthuma kwezempilo; ukuqinisekisa abantu abagcindezelekile nabasezinweni zokuhlupheka babenokungena okukhonekayo,okufanele nemisebenzi yokuhlumelelisa isimilo efanele okufaka ukusiza kwezobuchwepheshe; gwema ukubiza okungaphandle kwephakethe kubantu ukuthola imisebenzi yokuhlunyeleliswa kwesimilo kanye nokusizwa kwezobuchwepheshe okungadala ubunzima kwezezemali; futhi ukubhekana nezinga elinganele lamanje lokusebenza kokuhlunyeleliswa kwesimilo ukusiza izidingo zesibalo sabantu. Uhla lwezinqumo lokwahlukana kwezenzo okumele zithathwe nguNobhala weWHO njenge:
translations.reference: ensure countries are better equipped to respond to the sudden increase in rehabilitation needs including assistive technology due to health emergencies; ensure persons in marginalized and vulnerable situations have access to affordable, quality and appropriate rehabilitation services including assistive technology; avoid high out-of-pocket costs for people to access rehabilitation services and assistive technology that can cause financial hardships; and address the current insufficient level of rehabilitation workforce to serve the needs of the population. The resolution lists a range of actions to be taken by the WHO Secretariat such as:
translations.hypothesis: ensuring that countries have the necessary resources to respond effectively to public health emergencies, including access to essential medicines and supplies; ensuring that vulnerable populations have access to essential health services, including vaccination and treatment; preventing the spread of misinformation and disinformation about public health, including promoting evidence-based health information; and addressing the current challenges in the delivery of public health services, including workforce shortages. The following decisions should be made by the WHO Director-General:

translations.source: Ukushicilela isizinda sombiko ekupheleni kuka-2026 ngolwazi ngamandla eMember States ukuphendula ezidingweni zokuhlunyeleliswa kwesimilo; ukudevelopha okuqondiwe nezinkomba ngokukhava okufanele kokuhlunyeleliswa kwesimilo ngo-2030; ukuqinisekisa izinsizakusebenza ezifanele ezizofakwa kuWHO ukweseka iMember States ekwenzeni ukulawula kwezobuchwepheshe kanye nezinsizakusebenza; futhi ukweseka iMember States ukuthi ihlanganise ukuhlunyeleliswa kwesimilo nokusiza kwezobuchwepheshe ekuzilungiseleliseni okuphuthumayo kanye nohlelo lwempendulo. Unobhala weWHO uzobika ngokuqhubeka kokwenza kwalesisinqumo eMnhlanganweni weZempilo ngo-2026, 2028 kanye no-2030.
translations.reference: publishing a baseline report by the end of 2026 with information on the capacity of Member States to respond to rehabilitation needs; developing targets and indicators for effective coverage of rehabilitation services by 2030; ensuring appropriate resources are allocated at WHO to support Member States in implementing technical guidance and resources; and supporting Member States to integrate rehabilitation and assistive technology in their emergency preparedness and response plan. The WHO Secretariat will report on progress in the implementation of this resolution to the Health Assembly in 2026, 2028 and 2030.
translations.hypothesis: The publication of a comprehensive report at the end of 2026, providing Member States with the necessary information and tools to implement the Global Strategy on Diabetes; the development of clear and measurable indicators for the implementation of the Global Strategy by 2030; the assurance that appropriate resources will be allocated to the WHO to support Member States in implementing the strategy; and the assurance that Member States will integrate diabetes prevention and control into their national health plans and emergency response systems. The Director-General of the WHO will report on the progress of these decisions at the World Health Assembly in 2026, 2028 and 2030.

translations.source: Idokhumenti ehlobene: EB152/8 - ukuqinisa ukuhlunyeleliswa kwesimilo kuzinhlelo zezempilo, umbiko ngu Mqondisi Jikelele Ilinki ehlobene Umsebenzi weWHO ekuhlunyelelisweni kwezimilo Izinto ngasenhla zadingidwa njengengxenye yedokhumenti A76/7 Rev.1 - Umbiko ohlanganisiwe nguMqondisi Jikelele. Isinqumo sokuqinisa amandla okuxilonga
translations.reference: Related document: EB152/8 - Strengthening rehabilitation in health systems, Report by the Director-General Related link WHO's work on rehabilitation The items above were discussed as part of the document A76/7 Rev.1 - Consolidated report by the Director-General. Resolution on strengthening diagnostics capacity
translations.hypothesis: Relevant documents: EB152/8 - Strengthening the integration of mental health into health systems, report by the Director-General of the World Health Organization, Mental health is considered an integral part of the document A76/7 Rev.1 - Consolidated report by the Director-General. Decision to strengthen surveillance

translations.source: Ngomhlaka-26 kuNhlaba iMember States bavume isinqumo sokuqinisa amandla okuxilongaemazweni nokwenzangcono ukungena emisebenzini yokuxilonga. Ububanzi bokwahlukana kwezinqumo kubona ukuthi imisebenzi yokuxilonga ibalulekile ekuvikeleni, ukubheka nokuxilonga, ukuphathwa kwecala, ukubheka nokulapha okuxhunyanwa ngakho, okungaxhunyanwa ngakho izifo ezinganakiwenezifo ezingajwayelekile, ukulimala nokukhubazeka. Ukuxilonga kuvumela ukucaca kokuhlonza izifo,ngakho ke ukuqala ngesikhathi kokwelasha okufanele kwemiphumela yezempilo engcono. Isinqumo sithatha ukwahluka okuphelele kokuxilongwa ngakho kuhlanganisa kokubili vitro ukuhlolwa kwaselebhu, isibonelo ukuphindwa phindwa kokuhlolwa kokuxilongwa kanye nokuvela kwesheni lepholierase (PCR) kanye nokunyona ivitro, isibonelo, ukuhlanganisa nomaumfutho wgazi namadivayisi okukala. Kukhava izenzo zokucwaninga nokuthuthuka ukukhiqiza (okufaka umkhiqizo wendawo nokudluliselwa kobuchwepheshe), ukulawula, ukukhetha nokuthenga, ukuqwashisa nokumela nokubhekana nezivimbo zokuthola ezijwayelekile.
translations.reference: On 26 May Member States endorsed a resolution to strengthen diagnostics capacity in countres and to improve access to diagnostic services. The broad ranging resolution recognizes that diagnostic services are vital for the prevention, surveillance, diagnosis, case management, monitoring and treatment of communicable, noncommunicable, neglected tropical and rare diseases, injuries, and disabilities. Diagnostics allow for the precise identification of diseases, and therefore the timely initiation of the correct treatments for better health outcomes. The resolution considers the full spectrum of “diagnostics”, thus including both “in vitro” laboratory tests e.g. rapid diagnostic tests and polymerase chain reaction (PCR) and “non in vitro” diagnostics e.g. imaging or blood pressure measurement devices. It covers actions for research and development, manufacturing (including local production and technology transfer), regulation, selection and procurement, awareness, advocacy and addressing access barriers in general.
translations.hypothesis: On November 26, the Member States agreed to strengthen the capacity for laboratory testing and to improve access to testing services. The decision reflects the importance of testing in preventing, detecting, and managing infectious diseases, as well as non-communicable diseases, and in addressing the health consequences of these diseases. Testing enables accurate diagnosis, which is essential for timely and appropriate treatment. The decision includes a comprehensive approach to testing, including both laboratory-based and point-of-care testing, such as repeat testing and the use of rapid diagnostic tests (RDTs) and molecular diagnostics (PCR), as well as in-vitro testing, such as the use of blood tests and other diagnostic devices. It also includes measures to support research and development (including local production and technology transfer), quality control, procurement, training, and the management of laboratory infrastructure.

translations.source: Ukwenza kwesinqumo kuzokwakha nokwandisa emsebenzini eyedlule neyamanje kumazinga amathathu eWHO ukusiza amazwe enze kangcono ukuthola ezokuxilongwa. Unobhala ucelwe ukuthi abike ngokuqhubeka kokwenza ngo-2025. Idokhumenti ehlobene EB152/6 Ukuqinisa amandla okuxilonga
translations.reference: The implementation of the resolution will build and expand on previous and current work at three levels of WHO to help countries improve access to diagnostic services. The Secretariat is requested to report on implementation progress in 2025. Related document EB152/6 Strengthening diagnostics capacity
translations.hypothesis: The decision to build and expand the existing and current laboratory facilities to three WHO standards will help countries improve their surveillance capabilities. The project is scheduled to be completed by 2025. The relevant document is EB152/6 Strengthening Surveillance Capacity

translations.source: Imiklomelo yokunikela okumangazayo lwezempilo lomphakathi eyethulwe NgoMhlangano weZempilo woMhlaba wamashumi ayisithupha nesithupha. Ngesikhathi somcimbi wokuthutha woMhlangano weZempilo woMhlaba wamashumi ayisithupha nesithupha obuseGeneva, imiklomelo yethulwe kubantu kanye nezikhungo emhlabeni wonke ngokunikela kwabo okumangazayo kwezempilo yomphakathi. "Imiklomelo yezempilo yomphakathi yango-2023 kanye nemiklomelo yokubungaza abantu baenze umehluko omkhulu kwezempilo yomhlaba, nomsebenzi wabo osudlule lokhu obekulindelekile," kusho uDkt Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, uMqondisi Jikelele weWHO. Ngesikhathi umhlaba ubhekene nezingqinamba eziningi,ngayinye iyisikhuthazi futhi njengesikhumbuzi sokuqhubeka esingenziwe ukwenzangcono ezempilo nokuphila kuwona wonke umuntu. Isimemezelo sokukhethwa kwezihambeli ngomklomelo ngamunye siyakhishwa unyaka ngamunye ngemva kokuvalwa koMhlangano weZempilo woMhlaba.
translations.reference: Awards for outstanding contributions to public health presented during the Seventy-sixth World Health Assembly During a moving ceremony at the Seventy-sixth World Health Assembly in Geneva, awards were presented to persons and institutions from around the world for their outstanding contributions to public health. “The 2023 public health prizes and awards celebrate people who have made a significant contribution to global health, and whose work has extended far beyond the call of normal duty,” said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO Director-General. “At a time when the world faces many challenges, each is an inspiration and a reminder of the progress that can be made to improve health and wellbeing for all.” The call for nominations of candidates for each prize is sent out each year after closure of the World Health Assembly.
translations.hypothesis: The World Health Organization's annual awards recognize individuals and organizations around the world for their outstanding contributions to public health. During the World Health Organization's annual awards ceremony in Geneva, awards were presented to individuals and organizations for their remarkable achievements in public health. "The 2023 World Health Organization Awards and the Laureates recognize individuals who have made a significant difference in the world of public health, and whose work has gone above and beyond what was expected," said Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Director-General of the WHO. As the world faces numerous challenges, these awards serve as a reminder of the progress that has been made in improving the health and well-being of people around the world. The selection of the award winners is made each year after the World Health Organization's annual meeting.

translations.source: Ukukhethwa kungenziwa abaphathi bezempilo bezwe beWHO Member State futhi noma yimuphi oke wathola umklomelo. Kuseshini yesi-152 yangoMasingana 2023, i-Executive Board yangesi-2023 abanqobi bemiklomelo, ngokwezimakamiso ezenziwe yiphaneli ekhethiwe ezimisele yomklomelo ngamunye. Abayisikhombisa abahlnishwe ngemiklomelo sebekhethiwe ukubungaza indima yabo ehlukile nokunikela kwezempilo yomphakathi emazweni abo nasemhlabeni wonke. Baqhamuka kuZifunda ezine zeWHO-kuma-America, Eastern-Mediterranean, South East Asia kanye neWestern Pacific. Abanqobile bahamba ngabodwa futhi abathathu babo ngabesifazane.
translations.reference: Nominations can be made by national health administrations of a WHO Member State and by any former recipient of the prizes. At its 152nd session in January 2023, the Executive Board designated the 2023 winners of the prizes, on the basis of proposals made by the dedicated selection panels for each prize. The seven laureates have been selected to celebrate their unique role and contribution for public health in their countries and globally. They come from four WHO Regions – for the Americas, the Eastern-Mediterranean, South-East Asia and the Western Pacific. Five winners are individuals, and three among them are women.
translations.hypothesis: The selection process can be undertaken by the health ministers of WHO Member States or any other individual who has been nominated. In the 152nd session of the Executive Board in January 2023, the Board approved a list of nominees for the awards, based on recommendations from a selection committee. The nominees represent four WHO regions: the Americas, Eastern Mediterranean, South-East Asia, and the Western Pacific. The nominees are all men, and three of them are women.

translations.source: Ukwengeza izikhungo ezimbili sezithole umklomelo wango 2023 obuholwa ngabesifazane. Abanqobi bemiklomelo ya-2023 Sasakawa Health Preize The 2023: iNick Simons Institute yaseNepa, noSolwazi Vichai Tiethavorn waseThailand. Isikhungo iNick Simons inhlangano ezimele ehlose ukwenzangcono ukunakekelwa kwezempilo ezindaweni zasemakhaya eNepal. Ukusebenzisana noNgqongqoshe weZempilo nesibalo sabantu waseNepal, isikhungo sesiqeqeshe izifundiswa zokwelapha ezingu-7000 (okungabahlengikazi ababelethisayo kanye nabaszi bezokuhlinza) ezindaweni zasemakhaya, manje abanikezela ngemisebenzi edingeka kakhulu emiphakathini yendawo ekungabini khona kwephuli eyanele yabasebenzi abajwayelekile kanye nongoti. Isikhungo sesinikele ngempahlakusebenza ebalulekile nokunikela ngabasebenzi bezempilo abengeziwe ukuqeda ukwentuleka kwezifundiswa zokwelapha ezibhedlela ezingu-44, futhi beseka ukwakhiwa kohlelo kwenza ukuze kube nokusebenza okufanele okungcono ezibhedlela zasemakhaya ezingaphezu kuka-100.
translations.reference: In addition, the two institutions that have received a 2023 award are led by women. Winners of the 2023 prizes Sasakawa Health PrizeThe 2023 laureates: The Nick Simons Institute from Nepal, and Professor Vichai Tienthavorn from Thailand The Nick Simons Institute is a nongovernmental organization aiming to improve health care in the rural areas of Nepal. Working closely with the Ministry of Health and Population of Nepal, the Institute has trained 7000 medical professionals (such as midwives and anaesthetic assistants) in rural areas, who now provide most-needed services to local communities in the absence of a sufficient pool of general practitioners and specialists. The Institute has supplied essential equipment and provided additional health personnel to alleviate the lack of medical professionals in 44 hospitals, and supported the design of an action plan for better quality services in more than 100 rural hospitals.
translations.hypothesis: The addition of two new organizations has resulted in two winners of the 2023 Sasakawa Health Prize. The 2023 winners are the Nick Simons Institute in Nepal and Solwazi Vichai Tiethavorn from Thailand. The Nick Simons Institute is a non-profit organization dedicated to improving healthcare in rural areas of Nepal. By working with the Ministry of Health and Population of Nepal, the institute has trained 7,000 healthcare workers (including doctors, nurses, and community health volunteers) to provide essential healthcare services in rural communities. The institute has also made significant contributions to the reduction of maternal mortality rates in Nepal, and has helped to establish a network of over 100 functional community health centers.

translations.source: Isikhungo siphinde senze ucwaningo nemisebenzi yokumela ukwakha imicabango nomthelela kwinqubomgomo enomthelela kubasebenzi bezempilo yasemakhaya eNepal. USolwazi Vichai Tienthavorn unguMongameli wePraborormarajchanok Institute NjengoNgqongqoshe weZempilo yoMphakathi eThailand. Eminye yemizamo esemhlabeni wonke isiholele ekwenzeni kabusha inqubomgomo yokugqugquzela ezempilo, ngalomsebenzi oletha umphumela wokuthatha inqubomgomo yezwe esebenzayo yokuvikela ithalassemia nokuyilawula. Uphinde wakha indlela enhle yethuluzi lokuxhumana nendlela yokuhlola ukusiza izakhamizi zijwayele ukuziphatha kwazo ukuze zivikele nokulawula ushukela nehayi hayi. Njengomphumela walomsebenzi, inqubomgomo yezwe yathathwa ngomthelela omuhle obalulekile emthwalweni wesifo sezwe.
translations.reference: The Institute also conducts research and advocacy activities to shape perceptions and influence policies that affect rural health workers in Nepal. Professor Vichai Tienthavorn is President of the Praboromarajchanok Institute at the Ministry of Public Health of Thailand. One of the nation-wide initiatives he has led is an innovative health promotion policy to help address thalassemia, with his work resulting in the adoption of an effective national policy for thalassemia prevention and control. He also designed an effective communications tool and screening method to help citizens to adapt their behaviours in order to prevent and control diabetes and hypertension. As a result of this work, a national policy was adopted with significant positive impact on the country’s disease burden.
translations.hypothesis: The center also conducted research and developed innovative strategies for implementing a comprehensive and evidence-based approach to community-based healthcare in Nepal. Dr. Vichai Tienthavorn, President of the Praborormarajchanok Institute and former Minister of Public Health in Thailand, is a leading figure in global efforts to reform healthcare systems. His work has contributed to the development of a national strategy for preventing and managing thalassemia, which has had a significant impact on the health of the Nepali people. He has also created a user-friendly tool for self-testing, which helps individuals to understand their own condition and take appropriate action to protect themselves from the disease. As a result of this work, the national health system has been strengthened and improved, leading to better health outcomes for the Nepali population.

translations.source: Usolwazi Tienthavorn iphinde yadlala indawo ebalulenkile ekubhekaneni nengqinamba yokuhanjiswa okungalingani komsebenzi wezempilo, ikakhulukazi kumngcele nasezindaweni zasemakhaya aseThailand. Wahola okwakuqaliwe kokubambisana nezikole zasemakhaya ukuheha abafundi basezikolweni zamazinga aphezulu, baqeqeshwe nokubasiza bazinze ezindaweni zasekhaya emva kokuqeda ukuqhutshekwa kohlelokusebenza lwezokulapha. Ukuhlonishwa ngemiklomelo kwemiklomelo 2023 eUnited Arab Emirates Health Foundation: uDkt Maria Asuncion Silverstre wasePhilippinesln wengeze ngabelaphayo abahloniphekayo nabacwaningi, uDkt Maria Asuncion Silverstre uyakuthokozela ukumela okwehlukile kokuncelisa ibele kanye nomsunguli weKalusugan ng Mag-Ina (Ezempilo zamama nengane) inhlangano ezimele yasePhilippine. UDkt Silvestre unamandla ngesipiliyonin sakhe njengomsebenzi womphakathi owenza ucwaningo futhi wenza indlela yokwenza ukusiza ukwenza ngcono ezempilo yomama kanye nezingane ezizelwe. Ingqalabutho yendlela yokwenza ayenzile uchaza isethi elula yokwenza okumele ithathwe ngabasebenzi bezempilo ababhekana nomama ngesikhathi sokubeletha nokunakekelwa kwengane yakhe emva kokuzalwa.
translations.reference: Professor Tienthavorn also played an essential role in addressing the challenge of uneven distribution of the health workforce, especially in border and rural areas of Thailand. He led the establishment of partnerships with village schools to recruit high-school students, train them and help them settle back locally after having accomplished an accelerated medical curriculum. United Arab Emirates Health Foundation Prize2023 award laureate: Dr Maria Asuncion Silvestre from the PhilippinesIn addition to being a renowned clinician and researcher, Dr Maria Asuncion Silvestre is a passionate advocate for exclusive breastfeeding and the founder of Kalusugan ng Mag-Ina (Health of Mother and Child) – a Philippine non-governmental organization. Dr Silvestre has leveraged her experience as a community practitioner to conduct research and design a protocol to help improve the health of mothers and newborns. The pioneering protocol she created describes a simple set of actions to be undertaken by health workers attending a mother during delivery and her newborn after birth.
translations.hypothesis: Dr. Tienthavorn has been working in a challenging environment, balancing the demands of both academic research and practical healthcare, particularly in rural areas of Thailand. She has been instrumental in fostering collaboration between schools and local healthcare providers to support students who are transitioning from secondary education to independent living, providing them with the skills and resources they need to succeed after graduation. She was honored with the 2023 Health Foundation Award in the United Arab Emirates for her outstanding contributions to public health. Dr. Maria Asuncion Silverstre, a leading researcher and advocate for maternal and child health in the Philippines, praised Dr. Tienthavorn's work as an example of how to effectively address the unique challenges faced by young people in rural communities. Dr. Silverstre highlighted the importance of providing comprehensive support to young people, including access to healthcare, education, and social services.

translations.source: Indlela yokwenza esebenzayo kukhona konke ukuhlela kuzona zonke izinsizakusebenza,isiza ukudlula isikhala sokulingana kwezempilo ekunakekelweni kwamama nengane. Ekubambisaneni nohulumeni wezwe lasePhilippine kanye namazwe angu-17 Ewho Western Pacific Region, usedlale indima ebaluleke kakhulu ekunyuseni umkhankaso wokwamukela kuqala wokusheshe kunakekelwe umntwana ozelwe - isethi yesibonelo iWHO - eliphakamisile nokungenelela okubiza kahle. Ingonyama Sheikh Sabah Al-Ahmad Al-Jaber Al-Sabah umklomelo wokucwaninga kwezokunakekelwa kwezempilo kwabantu abadala nokugqugquzela ezempilo. 2023 nomklomelo wabahlonishwe ngemiklomelo: Isizinda sezwe sokulawulwa kwezifo ezingamahlalakhona nezifo okungaxhunyanwa ngazo nokuvikelwa okuphuma eChina noDkt Abla Mehio Sabai waseLebanon. Isizinda sezwe sezifo ezingamahlalakhona nokulawula izifo okungaxoxwa ngazo nokuzivikela, ingxenye yesizinda samaChinese yokulawula nokuvikela izifo (China CDC) isisingathe umnyango wokugxila kwezempilo yabantu abadala kusukela ngo-2013.
translations.reference: This protocol, which is applicable in all resource settings, helps to bridge the health equity gap in mother-and-child care. In partnership with the national government of the Philippines as well as 17 countries in the WHO Western Pacific Region, she has played a key role in scaling up the First Embrace campaign for Early Essential Newborn Care – a set of simple WHO-recommended and cost-effective interventions.
